note: INDIAN hiring-compliance ruleset for HireGuard. Converted from the US ruleset on 2026-06-24 by Member C (Harsh). Citations are real Indian statutes / constitutional articles. category_default is the starting severity; @RiskScorer and @Counsel may adjust based on evidence. TODO(Member B): your seed.py + migrations.sql still reference 'US-FED' as the always-include jurisdiction — change that to 'India-Central'. TODO(Member A): the sample packets and intake.md prompt still use US locations/state codes — convert them to Indian context (INR, Indian cities/states).
jurisdiction_model: India: most employment-equality law is Union/Central (applies everywhere). Use 'India-Central' for the always-include set; state codes (e.g. 'KA', 'MH', 'DL', 'TN') for state-specific rules.

rules.title: Gender-coded or gender-restrictive language
rules.citation: Constitution of India, Arts. 15 & 16; Code on Wages, 2019, § 3 (no gender discrimination in recruitment and conditions of employment)
rules.recommendation_template: Use gender-neutral language (e.g. 'salesperson', 'they'). Do not restrict applications by sex; recruitment must be gender-neutral under the Code on Wages, 2019.

rules.title: Equal remuneration for same or similar work
rules.citation: Code on Wages, 2019, §§ 3-4 (equal pay; prohibition of gender discrimination in wages); erstwhile Equal Remuneration Act, 1976, § 4
rules.recommendation_template: Offer the same pay scale for the same or similar work regardless of gender. State a single role-based pay band.

rules.title: Caste, religion, or community-based restriction
rules.citation: Constitution of India, Arts. 15(1) & 16(2) (no discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, place of birth); Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989
rules.recommendation_template: Remove any caste, religion, or community preference. Selection must be based solely on job-related merit.

rules.title: Marital status, pregnancy, or family-planning inquiry
rules.citation: Maternity Benefit Act, 1961 (as amended 2017); Constitution of India, Art. 15
rules.recommendation_template: Do not ask about marital status, pregnancy, or family plans, and do not use them as hiring criteria.

rules.title: Disability discrimination / non-essential physical requirements
rules.citation: Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016, §§ 20-21 (non-discrimination and equal opportunity in employment)
rules.recommendation_template: State physical demands only where essential to the role, and add that reasonable accommodation is provided, as required by the RPwD Act, 2016.

rules.title: Exclusion of transgender / third-gender applicants
rules.citation: Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019, § 3(b) (prohibits discrimination in employment)
rules.recommendation_template: Allow all gender identities to apply; the Transgender Persons Act, 2019 prohibits employment discrimination based on gender identity.

rules.title: Health / HIV status / intrusive medical-test requirement
rules.citation: HIV and AIDS (Prevention and Control) Act, 2017, § 3 (prohibits discrimination); general medical-privacy norms
rules.recommendation_template: Do not require HIV testing or broad medical disclosures. Limit any medical check to bona-fide, role-essential fitness requirements.

rules.title: Arbitrary age bar or age-coded language
rules.citation: Constitution of India, Art. 14 (protection against arbitrariness); note: India has no general private-sector age-discrimination statute, so this is treated as a fairness / best-practice risk
rules.recommendation_template: Avoid arbitrary age limits or age-coded words; describe required skills and experience instead.

rules.title: Domicile, region, or native-language restriction
rules.citation: Constitution of India, Art. 16(2) (no discrimination on grounds of residence/place of birth for State employment); fairness norm for private employers
rules.recommendation_template: Replace blanket 'locals only' / native-language demands with the actual job-related language proficiency the role requires.

rules.title: Subjective scorecard criteria with disparate-impact risk
rules.citation: Constitution of India, Art. 14 (equality and non-arbitrariness in selection); general fairness principles
rules.recommendation_template: Replace subjective 'culture fit' criteria with job-related, behaviorally-anchored competencies applied uniformly to all candidates.
